title: ophrys × gauthieri subsp. fenarolii
intro: Ophrys × gauthieri subsp. fenarolii, also known as Fenaroli's bee orchid, is a hybrid orchid species derived from the cross between Ophrys gauthierii and Ophrys fusca. It is a rare and unique plant, often used in traditional medicine due to its purported medicinal properties. The plant contains iridoid glycosides, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, potentially providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Fenaroli's bee orchid can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has been traditionally used to support immune system function and reduce stress.
